column1: # mapping_set_title: Open Policy Agent (OPA) to ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Mappings
# mapping_set_description: Schema-level mappings between Open Policy Agent (OPA)
#   LinkML classes and ISO/IEC 27001:2022 ISMS LinkML classes. OPA policies
#   are frequently the executable form of an information-security policy or
#   topic-specific policy, and OPA bundles function as documented information
#   subject to the ISO 27001 documented-information lifecycle. Predicates -
#   skos:closeMatch where the OPA class is a direct executable analogue of an
#   ISO 27001 governance artefact (e.g. a Rego Policy implementing an
#   the OPA class only partially overlaps (e.g. an AuthorizationInput as an
#   evaluation invocation of a SecurityControl, or a BuiltinMetadataCatalog
#   grouped under a ControlCategory); skos:broadMatch where the OPA class is
#   a broader container that subsumes multiple ISO 27001 documents (e.g. a
#   bundle Manifest aggregating many documented-information items).

column10: A Rego policy compiled to an IR Policy is the executable expression of an ISO 27001 information-security policy when used in that governance frame
A Rego policy is most often a topic-specific policy (access control, admission, data sharing) executable form
An AuthorizationInput captures the runtime invocation context of an access-control SecurityControl from Annex A
A bundle Manifest packages signed, versioned policy artefacts that are documented-information instances in the ISO 27001 sense
The catalog of built-in categories groups primitives by domain, analogous to grouping controls by category at the schema-level
